svg.title: Token Economics Cost Latency Ladder
svg.desc: A schematic ladder showing how fast, think, tool, cache, and batch paths differ by latency, compute, price scope, and blocked claims.
svg.text: Cost, Latency, And The Service Contract
The same request can travel different economic paths.
Planning asset A-0225. Schematic: no exact latency, savings, hidden-budget, margin, or live-price claim.
Path
What changes
Reader-visible promise
What stays blocked
Fast path
Short prompt, ordinary output, minimal extra search.
Lower visible delay, simpler service contract.
Exact latency, margin, quality, utilization.
Think path
More samples, search, verifier work, or reasoning budget.
Potentially more inference work before answer.
Hidden budget, solved reasoning, cost curve.
Tool path
Calls tools, retrieves evidence, executes bounded actions.
More steps, more audit surfaces, more failure modes.
Autonomy, productivity, secure output.
Cache path
Repeated context may use a cached-input lane.
Meter may distinguish input from cached input.
Hit rate, guaranteed discount, margin.
Batch path
User accepts delayed processing or different priority.
Different price scope and latency contract.
Equivalent standard price, universal savings.
Caption contract
Use this ladder to explain service-contract choices, not to plot a measured frontier. Pair any exact price with provider, model, source, snapshot date, and tier.
Blocked
live prices, exact latency, actual production routing, hidden reasoning budget, provider margin, cost per token, price-quality frontier, productivity.
